meta.title: Unit 3 — MS Excel for Legal Data Management
meta.subtitle: Computer Information · Law students
meta.author: Academic Content Factory

slides.title: Unit 3
slides.subtitle: MS Excel for Legal Data Management
slides.notes: Teach Unit 3 as a modular block (6 hours).

slides.title: Unit 3 — MS Excel for Legal Data Management
slides.notes: Six hours, 10% weight. Use a realistic chambers case register. Emphasize client data privacy during demos.

slides.title: Excel for Legal Data Management
bullets: Turns scattered case facts into manageable registers
Workbook = .xlsx file; worksheet = single grid tab
Structure columns before entering many rows
Power comes from formulas, filters, and charts
Supports case tracking, client lists, and billing
Lab: case data sheet and billing sheet with formulas
slides.notes: Frame Excel as a professional case-management tool, not just a calculator. Define workbook vs worksheet early—common exam distinction.

slides.title: Data Entry and Formatting
bullets: Design headers: CaseID, Client, Court, Status, FeesDue
Format dates as dates; currency as number/currency
Freeze top row so headers stay visible when scrolling
Wrap text for long notes; avoid unnecessary merges
One row per case or transaction — no blank spacer rows
Use Data Validation to limit Status dropdown values
slides.notes: Show a Case Register column layout. Merged cells break sorting—warn students explicitly.

slides.title: Formulas and Functions
bullets: Every formula begins with = and uses cell references
=SUM(H2:H100) — total fees across matters
=AVERAGE() — mean value for reporting
=IF(G2="Pending","Follow-up","OK") — status helper
=COUNTIF(G:G,"Pending") — count pending cases
Lookup (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP): find client detail from master sheet
slides.notes: Students must write formulas by hand in exams. Practice SUM, IF, and COUNTIF on a sample case register. Introduce lookup conceptually.

slides.title: Sorting and Filtering Data
bullets: Sort orders rows by date, client name, or fee amount
Filter shows only rows matching criteria (e.g., Pending)
AutoFilter arrows appear on header row
Multiple filters narrow to urgent matters quickly
Clear filters before applying new analysis
Interpret filtered views in scenario exam questions
slides.notes: Demo filtering Status = Pending and sorting by NextDate. Exam scenarios may ask what a filter reveals about caseload.

slides.title: Charts and Graphs
bullets: Visual summary of numeric data — bar, pie, line charts
Select data range → Insert Chart → choose type
Bar charts compare case counts by court or status
Pie charts show proportion — use sparingly
Label axes and title for courtroom or seminar clarity
Charts update when underlying data changes
slides.notes: Link chart choice to message: compare categories with bar; show trend over time with line. Keep charts simple and readable.

slides.title: Case Tracking and Client Records
bullets: Case register: filing date, next date, status, advocate
Client master sheet: contact, matter history, notes
Limitation reminders via date columns and IF formulas
Separate sensitive client data — access controls matter
Regular backups of active registers
Never share full client sheets on unsecured channels
slides.notes: Privacy reminder throughout. A case tracker is the core lab deliverable—include NextDate and Status columns with formulas.

slides.title: Billing and Documentation Sheets
bullets: Columns: Date, Client, Description, Hours, Rate, Amount
Amount = Hours × Rate using formulas, not typed values
SUM row for total billable fees per period
Separate sheets for invoices vs payment received
Currency format and clear period labels (month/year)
Documentation sheet cross-links to case IDs
slides.notes: Billing lab: build a sheet where Amount is formula-driven. Pitfall: typing totals instead of using SUM.

slides.title: Unit 3 — Exam Tips
bullets: Define workbook vs worksheet with examples
Write SUM, IF, and COUNTIF formulas from a scenario
Explain what a filter on Status = Pending shows
Interpret a simple bar chart of case counts
Pitfall: typing values where formulas are required
Pitfall: merged cells breaking sort and filter
slides.notes: Formula writing is practical-exam core. Revise cell references and three essential functions. Practice reading a filtered register.

slides.title: Unit 3 — Next steps
bullets: Complete unit labs
Attempt unit Q&A bank
Submit unit assignment
Revise using revision sheet
slides.notes: Point students to materials/labs and materials/qa for this unit.
